favorites.author: Gabriel Garcia Marquez
favorites.quote: A reader lives a thousand lives before he dies.

favorites.author: Jorge Luis Borges
favorites.quote: I have always imagined that paradise will be a kind of library.

favorites.author: Isabel Allende
favorites.quote: Not all those who wander are lost.

favorites.author: Pablo Neruda
favorites.quote: It is our choices that show what we truly are.

favorites.author: Julio Cortazar
favorites.quote: One has to be careful of books and what is inside them.

favorites.author: Mario Vargas Llosa
favorites.quote: There is no friend as loyal as a book.

favorites.author: Octavio Paz
favorites.quote: A room without books is like a body without a soul.

favorites.author: Eduardo Galeano
favorites.quote: The only thing you absolutely have to know, is the location of the library.

favorites.author: Laura Esquivel
favorites.quote: Reading is dreaming with open eyes.

favorites.author: Carlos Fuentes
favorites.quote: Books are a uniquely portable magic.

favorites.author: J.K. Rowling
favorites.quote: We read to know we are not alone.

favorites.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
favorites.quote: Today a reader, tomorrow a leader.

favorites.author: Stephen King
favorites.quote: Once you learn to read, you will be forever free.

favorites.author: Agatha Christie
favorites.quote: Reading gives us someplace to go when we have to stay where we are.

favorites.author: Jane Austen
favorites.quote: A book is a gift you can open again and again.

favorites.author: Mark Twain
favorites.quote: So many books, so little time.

favorites.author: Ernest Hemingway
favorites.quote: The more that you read, the more things you will know.

favorites.author: Antoine de Saint-Exupery
favorites.quote: Reading is to the mind what exercise is to the body.

favorites.author: Lewis Carroll
favorites.quote: Books are the quietest and most constant of friends.

favorites.author: Roald Dahl
favorites.quote: A good book has no ending.

favorites.author: Virginia Woolf
favorites.quote: You cannot find peace by avoiding life.

favorites.author: Oscar Wilde
favorites.quote: It is what you read when you don't have to that determines what you will be when you can't help it.

favorites.author: Franz Kafka
favorites.quote: A book must be the axe for the frozen sea inside us.

favorites.author: Leo Tolstoy
favorites.quote: All great literature is one of two stories: a man goes on a journey or a stranger comes to town.

favorites.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
favorites.quote: The soul is healed by being with children.

favorites.author: Victor Hugo
favorites.quote: To learn to read is to light a fire.

favorites.author: Charles Dickens
favorites.quote: No one is useless in this world who lightens the burdens of another.

favorites.author: William Shakespeare
favorites.quote: What's in a name? That which we call a rose by any other name would smell as sweet.

favorites.author: Gustave Flaubert
favorites.quote: Read in order to live.

favorites.author: Marcel Proust
favorites.quote: The real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new landscapes, but in having new eyes.

favorites.author: Albert Camus
favorites.quote: In the midst of winter, I found there was, within me, an invincible summer.

favorites.author: Simone de Beauvoir
favorites.quote: I am too intelligent, too demanding, and too resourceful for anyone to be able to take charge of me entirely.

favorites.author: Maya Angelou
favorites.quote: There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.

favorites.author: Toni Morrison
favorites.quote: If you have some power, then your job is to empower somebody else.

favorites.author: Neil Gaiman
favorites.quote: A book is a dream that you hold in your hand.

favorites.author: C.S. Lewis
favorites.quote: You can never get a cup of tea large enough or a book long enough to suit me.

favorites.author: Ray Bradbury
favorites.quote: You don't have to burn books to destroy a culture. Just get people to stop reading them.

favorites.author: George Orwell
favorites.quote: Not all books are meant to be read, just some of them.

favorites.author: Haruki Murakami
favorites.quote: If you only read the books that everyone else is reading, you can only think what everyone else is thinking.

favorites.author: Gabriel Garcia Marquez
favorites.quote: He was still too young to know that the heart's memory eliminates the bad and magnifies the good.

favorites.author: Jaime Saenz
favorites.quote: If you meet the nymph, do not tell her I wept. Tell her you saw me in the rivers, washing gold for her chest.

favorites.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
favorites.quote: When you have eliminated the impossible, whatever remains, however improbable, must be the truth.

favorites.author: Alexandre Dumas
favorites.quote: All human wisdom is summed up in two words: wait and hope.

favorites.author: Jules Verne
favorites.quote: Science, my lad, is made up of mistakes, but they are mistakes which it is useful to make, because they gradually lead to the truth.

favorites.author: Dan Brown
favorites.quote: Men go to far greater lengths to avoid what they fear than to obtain what they desire.

favorites.author: Stieg Larsson
favorites.quote: There are no innocents. There are, however, different degrees of responsibility.

favorites.author: Hermann Hesse
favorites.quote: The bird fights its way out of the egg. The egg is the world. Who would be born must first destroy a world.

favorites.author: Jack London
favorites.quote: You can't wait for inspiration. You have to go after it with a club.

favorites.author: Kurt Vonnegut
favorites.quote: We are what we pretend to be, so we must be careful about what we pretend to be.

favorites.author: Isaac Asimov
favorites.quote: The true delight is in the finding out rather than in the knowing.

favorites.author: H.G. Wells
favorites.quote: Human history becomes more and more a race between education and catastrophe.

favorites.author: Paulo Coelho
favorites.quote: When you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you to achieve it.

favorites.author: Umberto Eco
favorites.quote: To survive, you must tell stories.

favorites.author: Aldous Huxley
favorites.quote: The more you know, the more you see.

favorites.author: Mikhail Bulgakov
favorites.quote: Cowardice is the greatest sin.

favorites.author: George R.R. Martin
favorites.quote: The best fantasy is written in the language of dreams.

favorites.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
favorites.quote: Never confuse a single defeat with a final defeat.

favorites.author: John Steinbeck
favorites.quote: I wonder how many people I've looked at all my life and never seen.

favorites.author: Jack Kerouac
favorites.quote: One day I will find the right words, and they will be simple.

favorites.author: Philip Roth
favorites.quote: The road to hell is paved with works-in-progress.

favorites.author: Cormac McCarthy
favorites.quote: You never know what worse luck your bad luck has saved you from.

favorites.author: Margaret Atwood
favorites.quote: A word after a word after a word is power.

favorites.author: William Golding
favorites.quote: The greatest ideas are the simplest.

favorites.author: Edgar Allan Poe
favorites.quote: All that we see or seem is but a dream within a dream.

favorites.author: D.H. Lawrence
favorites.quote: Be still when you have nothing to say; when genuine passion moves you, say what you've got to say, and say it hot.

favorites.author: Sylvia Plath
favorites.quote: I took a deep breath and listened to the old brag of my heart: I am, I am, I am.
